behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior depends on wiring file discovery, filename parsing/sorting, and migration application together, so unit-only sort tests can miss handoff bugs. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP do not force higher than Integration. Rejected System because behavior text only requires local directory + DB ordering logic, not subprocess/real network/external API contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Run the runner against a test migrations directory with out-of-order filenames (e.g., 001, 003, 002) and a local test Postgres DB; verify applied order via DB effects and/or _migrations rows is 001->002->003.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: idempotency is about interaction between metadata persistence (_migrations) and execution control across runs. Unit tests of skip logic alone can miss persistence/query integration defects. Rejected System because behavior text is about repeated local runner+DB interaction, not subprocess/OS/network-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute runner twice against the same local test DB and migration set; assert first run applies migration and records it, second run performs no re-execution (no duplicate side effects, no duplicate metadata rows).

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure handling spans migration executor, transaction handling, and run-loop stop conditions. Unit tests cannot fully validate module interaction. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no LLM and no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text does not require subprocess/OS/network effects; it can be validated with a real local Postgres transaction and readback.
behaviors.test_description: Use local test DB with migration N containing a deliberate SQL error after a prior statement in the same transaction and migration N+1 as valid; run runner and assert N leaves no persisted partial changes (rollback) and N+1 is not attempted.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires concurrent execution from separate processes. This is OS/subprocess scheduling plus DB locking/constraint behavior that mocks often hide. System Triad applies via externally visible exactly-once side effect across processes.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two real runner processes concurrently against the same test Postgres DB and identical migrations; after both exit, verify each migration appears once in _migrations and data side effects exist exactly once.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the _migrations table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks orchestration between bootstrap metadata-table creation and migration execution order, which spans modules. Unit tests on "create table if missing" are insufficient. Rejected System because no subprocess/network/external API behavior is required; local DB integration readback is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Against a fresh local test DB, run runner with at least one migration and verify _migrations exists before recording/applying migration effects (e.g., via metadata writes and successful first migration application without missing-table errors).
